patterns.whyItWorks: Metadata becomes reviewable and reusable before automation starts mutating surrounding systems.
patterns.whereItFails: A catalog becomes stale busywork when ownership and remediation paths are not explicit.
patterns.whenToUseIt: Use when Playbook needs to standardize repo truth across multiple consumer repos.

patterns.whyItWorks: Schemas, examples, and validators lock the artifact boundary before CLI behavior expands.
patterns.whereItFails: Command-first rollouts reverse-engineer artifacts later and drift under pressure.
patterns.whenToUseIt: Use for research, scorecard, template, and Lifeline manifest additions.

patterns.whyItWorks: It does not; it only creates adoption theater around an untrusted shell.
patterns.whereItFails: The UI arrives before scoreable evidence, reusable contracts, or stable remediation hooks.
patterns.whenToUseIt: Avoid this when deciding whether to add a Playbook research command before contract truth exists.
